text: This is Said Arfanian presenting video of robotic repair of canal of noxious. The patient is a twenty year old healthy woman complaining of the right groin bulge and discomfort with prominent bulge on the exam. On CT scan had inguinal hernia with canal of noxious that extends into labia.
The surgeon accesses the abdominal cavity and identifies a partially reducible hernia on the right side. The contralateral side shows normal anatomy with clearly visible anatomical structures.

The surgeon identifies the direct and indirect hernia areas in relation to the epigastric vessels. A peritoneal flap is created using scissors with cautery, advancing the flap inferiorly and laterally towards the hernia defect. The inferior epigastric vessels are visible in the periperitoneal plane.

Upon reaching the defect, the surgeon releases the cyst neck circumferentially and begins reducing the lobulated cyst using scissors and cautery. After partial reduction, the right arm is switched to a needle holder for improved handling and to prevent rupture of the fragile, multilobulated cyst, which is visible in the surgical field.

The surgeon completes the reduction of the cyst, which is connected to the surrounding tissue through the canal of nuke. The canal is dissected towards the plutonal side to prevent cyst rupture. After cyst removal, a permanent polypropylene mesh is placed to decrease recurrence risk, and it is secured to the soft tissue with absorbable sutures.
The surgeon closes the paternal flap using a barbed suture, which is also utilized to close the defect at the neck of the cyst dissection.
